# terrible_community_vmware_vcenter_standard_key_provider (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) Name of the Key Provider to be added, reconfigured or removed from vCenter.

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `kms_info` (String) The information of an external key server (KMS). `kms_info[].kms_name`, `kms_info[].kms_ip` are required when adding a Standard Key Provider. If `kms_info[].kms_port` is not specified, the default port 5696 will be used. `kms_info[].kms_ip`, `kms_info[].kms_port` can be reconfigured for an existing KMS with name `kms_info[].kms_name`.
- `kms_password` (String) Password to authenticate to the KMS.
- `kms_username` (String) Username to authenticate to the KMS.
- `make_kms_trust_vc` (String) After adding the Standard Key Provider to the vCenter Server, you can establish a trusted connection, the exact process depends on the certificates that the key provider accepts, and on your company policy. Three methods implemented here, (1) upload client certificate and private key through `make_kms_trust_vc.upload_client_cert` and `make_kms_trust_vc.upload_client_key` parameters, (2) generate, update, download vCenter self signed certificate through `make_kms_trust_vc.download_self_signed_cert` parameter, (3) download generated Certificate Signing Request(CSR) through `make_kms_trust_vc.download_client_csr` parameter, send it to KMS then upload the KMS signed CSR through `make_kms_trust_vc.upload_kms_signed_client_csr` parameter. This is not set to be mandatory, if not set, please go to vCenter to setup trust connection with KMS manually.
- `mark_default` (Boolean) Set specified Key Provider with name `name` as the default Key Provider. If new added Key Provider is the only key provider in vCenter, then will mark it as default after adding.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `proxy_port` (Number) Port of the proxy server.
- `proxy_server` (String) Address of the proxy server to connect to KMS.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) If set to `absent`, the named Key Provider will be removed from vCenter. If set to `present`, the named existing Key Provider will be reconfigured or new Key Provider will be added.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `key_provider_clusters` (String) the Key Provider cluster info
